title: gh-126366: Fix crash if `__iter__` raises an exception during `yield from`
author: ZeroIntensity
body:
Apparently, gh-126366 has two issues: one is a thread safety problem inside `list.__init__`, and then the other is that the `GET_YIELD_FROM_ITER` instruction crashes if a non-native-generator object raises an exception in its `__iter__`. Since the latter only applies to 3.14, I'm splitting the fix into two PRs.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-126366 -->
* Issue: gh-126366
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: I don't understand how the JIT failures could be related.

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: @savannahostrowski @diegorusso My JIT experts, are these known failures, or is there something wrong with my fix?

comments.author: savannahostrowski
ZeroIntensity
comments.body: Thank you, Savannah! I'll update the skip list and see if that fixes it.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Would it be possible to backport the test to 3.12 and 3.13?

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: Maybe, but would it be all that useful? Anything that gets backported to those versions will (probably) have to go through the test on main anyway.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: It's useful to check for non-regression: make sure that we don't break this code path in the future.
